summary: A container build step failed because a wget command to download a nydus release archive exited with status 1 after remote fetches were canceled (network/context canceled).
root_cause: The build step running wget to fetch the nydus release archive failed (exit code 1) after remote fetches returned 'context canceled', causing the build to abort.
relevant_tests: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1

evidence.quote: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the exact command that failed and that it exited with a non-zero status (exit code 1).

evidence.quote: time="2026-05-06T19:55:17.4611288Z" level=debug msg="fetch failed" digest="sha256:0288b5a0d7e71a2cdbdf114cb9869ce2bc4b05adfd6aa5e3ee9fa4b3847e7b80" error="failed to do request: Get \"https://cdn01.quay.io/...\": context canceled"
evidence.reason: Records a remote layer fetch failing with 'context canceled', indicating network/transfer cancellation during the build.

evidence.quote: /moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = process "/bin/bash -c wget ..." did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Confirms the buildkit Solve RPC errored because the wget process failed, linking the fetch failure to the overall build failure.

evidence.quote: time="2026-05-06T19:55:17.4643579Z" level=error msg="/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"
evidence.reason: Shows the operation was ultimately canceled with 'context canceled', consistent with network/transfer cancellation rather than a compile/test error.

suggested_fix: Retry the job (transient network failures often resolve on retry). If failures recur, ensure the wget URL is reachable from the build environment (https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/... and quay.io endpoints) or host the nydus artifact in a reliably accessible location. Optionally add retry logic to the download step (e.g., wget --tries, curl --retry) to tolerate transient network glitches.

summary: A functional test failed because pip could not download a test package from GitHub: repeated 502 responses caused an OSError and the script returned exit code 1.
root_cause: pip failed to install the test package from GitHub due to repeated 502 responses (network error), causing the test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] to fail with an AssertionError when the script returned code 1.

evidence.reason: Shows the test failed because a script returned a non-zero exit code.
Shows the failing operation was pip installing a package from a GitHub URL.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Direct error message indicating repeated 502 responses from GitHub prevented the download.
Identifies the specific test that failed and was scheduled for rerun.
Captured stdout showing the exact pip command that failed.

suggested_fix: This appears to be a transient network/GitHub-side failure. Re-run the failing job first. If failures recur, make the test resilient to external network errors (for example, host the test package on a local test server or mock the download, or add retry/backoff handling for fetching GitHub archives) so tests don't depend on external GitHub availability.

summary: One Jest test failed because the expected registry URL string did not match the actual output: the actual value included a trailing slash.
root_cause: A test assertion in test/config/get.ts expected "https://my-org.registry.example.com" but received "https://my-org.registry.example.com/", causing an equality failure.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Shows the exact failing test name.
The test's expected value (no trailing slash).
The actual output has a trailing slash, causing the equality mismatch.
Pinpoints the assertion location in the test file.

suggested_fix: Either update the test to accept or normalize a trailing slash (for example trim a trailing '/' from stdout before comparing), or change the command/code that prints the registry URL so it consistently omits the trailing slash.
